Home / Services / Concrete Slabs

Concrete Slabs

Durable concrete slabs for sheds, garages, and more.

Need a solid, level surface for your shed, garage, or other outdoor structure? Groundworks specializes in pouring durable concrete slabs that provide a stable foundation for your projects. We understand the importance of a properly constructed slab, especially in Wichita, where soil conditions and weather patterns can present unique challenges.

Our team meticulously prepares the site, ensuring proper drainage and compaction. We use only high-quality concrete mixes, reinforced with steel mesh or fiber as needed, to provide exceptional strength and durability. We also pay close attention to the slope of the slab, ensuring that water drains away properly.

Whether you're building a new shed, garage, or other outdoor structure, Groundworks will work closely with you to ensure that your concrete slab is built to the highest standards. We handle everything from initial site preparation and excavation to final grading and cleanup. Our goal is to provide you with a solid foundation that will support your structure for years to come.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site Preparation and Excavation – We prepare the site by excavating and leveling the ground.
  2. 02 Step 2: Formwork Installation – We install the forms that will shape the concrete.
  3. 03 Step 3: Reinforcement Installation – We install steel mesh or fiber to reinforce the concrete.
  4. 04 Step 4: Concrete Pouring and Curing – We pour and cure the concrete to ensure its strength and durability.

Benefits

  • Stable Foundation
  • Increased Property Value
  • Durable and Long-Lasting
  • Protection from Moisture Damage

Frequently Asked Questions

How thick should a concrete slab be?

The thickness of a concrete slab depends on the intended use. A slab for a shed typically needs to be 4 inches thick, while a slab for a garage may need to be 5-6 inches thick.

What is the purpose of steel mesh in a concrete slab?

Steel mesh provides tensile strength to the concrete, preventing it from cracking under stress.

How long does it take for a concrete slab to cure?

Concrete typically takes 28 days to fully cure, but it can be loaded after 7 days.
